caption:
11 months of asking. One email. $3,000.

Inside a full-time role, your income grows through other people's calendars. Performance reviews. Budget cycles. Headcount freezes. You do the work. Someone else holds the lever.

The math of a portfolio career is different. Every new client is a raise you give yourself. You set the price. You sign the contract. You start next week, not next review cycle.

I know a fractional CMO who went from one job to two contracts and added $41,000 to her annual income in 2024. No performance review. No boss approval.

Your next raise is not behind a meeting. It's behind an email. What skill would you package first?
